revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五社神社 (池田市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Gosha Shrine (五社神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Gosya01.jpg | location = {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, Hachizuka 2-chome 4-28 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.817389|N|135.437167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Takeru Hayasusa-no-mikoto), {{ill|Itsukushima-no-mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima-no-mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10881556}}, Sumiyoshi Ōkami, Anaori Ōkami | establishment = Shinki 1 (724 CE) | festival = October 13 | location = {{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}} }}
'''Gosha Shrine''' (五社神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture, in the Hachizuka district.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Takeru Hayasusa-no-mikoto) * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Itsukushima-no-mikoto|ja|五十猛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Ōkami]] * Anaori Ōkami
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in 724 CE (Shinki 1) by the monk {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyoki|1=Gyōki}} as the guardian shrine for Tara-san Jakō-ji Temple. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, Jakō-ji joined the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, and a 13-tiered stone pagoda was erected inside the stone chamber of the adjacent {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}}, adopting the Five Wisdom Buddhas as its {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honji Suijaku|1=Honji suijaku}}, and the shrine came to be called Gosha Daimyōjin.
* In 1573 (Tenshō 1), the shrine and temple were destroyed during the wars of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}. * In 1589 (Tenshō 17), the shrine was rebuilt by the monk Denyo Shōnin of Shakain. * At the beginning of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, during the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the shrine was renamed Gosha Shrine. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. * On June 17, 1907 (Meiji 40), it was merged with the {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|lt=Susanoo Shrine|lt=Mukoyama Shrine|ja|素盞鳴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 素盞嗚神社}} in Nangaito and the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} in Koinoyama.<ref>Inoue Masao, "Osaka Prefecture Complete History, Volume 3" (1922) {{全国書誌番号|73012615}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda), tou.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|13-tiered stone pagoda}}]] * '''13-tiered stone pagoda at Gosha Shrine''' – Designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan (June 27, 1959). Constructed in the early {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. ''' The pagoda stands about 5 meters tall, with its base partially buried. The surface is divided into two sections, and the central tower is adorned with carvings of the seed syllables of the {{ill|Five Wisdom Buddhas|ja|金剛界五仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Wisdom Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五智如来|1=Five Tathāgatas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q265911}}. It was likely built during the period of {{ill|Kumano faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}}. It was first designated an Important Art Object in 1935 and was officially recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1959. * '''Artifacts from the Kofun atop Hachizuka Kofun''' – Designated as Ikeda City Cultural Properties, Archaeological Materials No. 2 (October 31, 1978), from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}.
== Access == * From {{ill|Ikeda Station|en|Ikeda Station|qid=Q1206276|lt=Ikeda Station (Osaka)|1=Ikeda Station}}, walk southeast for 20 minutes, or take the {{ill|Hankyu Bus|ja|阪急バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Bus|1=Hankyu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11656745}} city line to the {{ill|Sonbashi|ja|尊鉢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonbashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} stop and walk 5 minutes. * From {{ill|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|en|Ishibashi handai-mae Station|qid=Q866078|lt=Ishibashi Handai-mae Station|1=Ishibashi handai-mae Station}}, walk northwest for about 20 minutes, or take the Hankyu Bus city line to the {{ill|Mizutsuki Park stop|ja|水月公園前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizutsuki Park stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and walk 8 minutes, or to Sonbashi and walk 5 minutes.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Gosha-jinja (Ikeda)}} * {{ill|Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|ja|鉢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachizuka Kofun (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shaka-in Temple (Ikeda)|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaka-in Temple (Ikeda)|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|version=103218636|comment=Imported full ja history}}
